Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities For Order To Transfer Action And To Consolidate Actions In Superior Court [Ccp Section 404, 1003; Cal Rules Of Ct. 3.520(B)]
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Discount Plumbing, Inc.'s Motion For Order To Transfer Action And To Consolidate Actions is OFF CALENDAR.

Defendant has not demonstrated it timely served its motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(e) [service by fax only be agreement and adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Defendant served his motion by email on 4/17/2026, which is not 16 court days plus two court days before 5/11/2026, the hearing date noticed. Defendant's disregard for the rules is not excused.

A party seeking to have a motion heard outside the regular noticed period must secure an order shortening time or advancing the hearing. Defendant has no such order and, therefore, its (second) attempt to jump the line fails and his untimely motion is ordered off calendar.

Additionally, Moving Defendant has violated a written court order. In connection with its motion set for hearing on 4/20/2026, the court ordered Moving Defendant to submit promptly a proposed order verbatim the tentative ruling. Nearly three weeks later, Moving Defendant has not yet submitted the proposed order. Moving Defendant is ordered to submit the proposed order for the 4/20/2026 by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org no later than 8:30 am on 5/11/2026.

Prior to the hearing, Moving Defendant shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order repeating the above verbatim.

The Court signs two orders. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Plaintiff Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company's unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Jeffrey M. Zielinski Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Jeffrey M. Zielinski has failed to declare '[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) Counsel declares he "ha[s] not been admitted nor applied pro hac vice in the state of California in the past two years for any state Superior Court," Counsel's testimony begs the question whether he has applied to appear PHV in the state appellate courts? The clear intent of the requirement is to assure the court counsel is not seeking to appear PHV with such frequency that they should seek admission to the State Bar rather than PHV status. On this record the court cannot make the necessary determination. It is notable counsel's application was previously denied for failing to satisfy the requirements of rule 9.40, which are straightforward. The court is not inclined to consider an unlimited number of applications regarding the same counsel.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2025 Set For Trial On Nov-09-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - PLAINTIFF PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

Plaintiff Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company's unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Thaddeus S. Kirk Pro Hac Vice is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Thaddeus S. Kirk has failed to declare '[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (See Cal. Rules of Court, rule 9.40(d)(5).) It is not sufficient for counsel to simply state he or she has not been admitted pro hac vice in the last two years. The requirements are quite straightforward and set out clearly in the Rule of Court. Counsel should take care to fulfill them.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
